steps.title: Read the final image
steps.content: The poem compares humans to trapped animals and ends with a person described as a lofty soul wrapped in dreams and visions but trapped in the body. That image suggests ambition or aspiration limited by physical reality.

steps.title: Avoid literal misreads
steps.content: Choice A focuses on taming animals, but the poem uses the lion as a comparison to humans. Choice B invents a problem with attainable goals and motivation. Choice C invents frustration and lack of self-control. The remaining choice captures aspirations limited by constraints. The answer is D.
